statement: In 2024, Iceland's GDP per capita in purchasing power standards (PPS) was approximately €52,300, compared to the EU-27 average of €39,700. Among Nordic peers, Norway led at €64,500 (boosted by petroleum wealth), Denmark stood at €50,800, Sweden at €44,700, and Finland at €40,900. Iceland thus ranks second among the Nordics and approximately 32% above the EU average, though below Norway's level.
caveats: PPP-adjusted figures correct for price level differences but can be sensitive to methodology. Small open economies with volatile exchange rates may show more year-to-year variation. Norway's figure is heavily influenced by petroleum rents.

citations.report_title: 390.000 hektarar af landbúnaðarlandi breytast í skóg og votlendi

claims.claim_text: Hagkerfi Danmerkur hefur vaxið og lífskjör aukist á sama tíma og losun gróðurhúsalofttegunda dróst saman um tæp 45%.
